quote_of_day.text:
>>13943
>blah blah i am shut-in
nah you are not, you probably are another normie. real shut-ins are neets who genuinely dont go out even to just see the sky for once in days and days.
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of despondency moved over the board as Anon bid adieu, sparking a debate on Indiachan's decline.

The announcement was met with a cold snap of indifference, but some responded with a flurry of comments.

A scattered drizzle of incel angst fell in the afternoon, as one anon claimed to be incel and sought validation.

The post drew a gust of criticism, with replies characterising it as an attempt to garner sympathy.

A high-pressure ridge of educational cheating allegations formed over the Pajeet Babus, who were accused of banning Telegram to prevent cheating.

A front of frustration blew in, with some arguing that this was an ineffective solution to a deeper problem.

Meanwhile, a fog of speculation rolled in regarding BJP links to sex work and the Indian government's move on some unspecified issue, with various theories and concerns being aired.

articles.title: Anon Bids Adieu, Sparks Debate on Indiachan's Decline
articles.body:
Anon announced their departure from Indiachan, stating, 'You all lost single quality poster of this bhangi forum.' One responding anon expressed concern that the departure of a quality poster like IAC anon could be a sign of the forum's decline.

They noted that despite IAC anon's contributions, they received nothing in return, suggesting that the forum may have reached a low point.

Another anon directly addressed IAC anon, questioning why they would seek attention from the forum when they could share their skills elsewhere.

This poster suggested that IAC anon was better off leaving the 'shithole' and joining a community of 'real talented people.' Some anons seemed indifferent or even welcoming to IAC anon's potential departure.

One anon simply stated, 'Kek the board is dead anyways. Good riddance.' Others made comments like 'couldnt care less' or 'Toh hum kya kare bahinchod,' reflecting a mix of apathy and frustration.

The discussion also touched on the perceived value of contributors to the forum.

One anon claimed that low-quality posters ('lows') were in control, while another suggested that quality posters were aware not to waste their time on the forum, which they referred to as 'chamarchan.' The thread revealed a divided community, with some anons lamenting the loss of a quality poster and others showing little concern or even celebrating their departure.

The debate highlighted tensions between posters about the forum's current state and its future prospects.

articles.title: Anon Claims to be Incel, Seeks Validation
articles.body:
Anon opened the discussion by mocking another poster who they claimed thought they looked cool talking about anime, gaming, and cats in front of non-online people.

The OP labeled this person an 'internet incel slop.' Another anon disputed the idea of a self-described shut-in, suggesting that real shut-ins are those who genuinely do not leave their homes for days, implying the original poster was not one.

One poster joked about being an incel, believing that discussing the topic could help them get a boyfriend.

A respondent reacted to this by distancing themselves, stating they were 'talking about girls, not a faggot.' The conversation quickly turned hostile, with one poster telling another they would 'get mocked and humiliated faggot.' A separate exchange involved an anon telling someone to 'kys' after they mentioned not talking to 'normlfags.' Some anons made brief, cryptic comments, such as 'you better rope' and 'based if true,' while one simply called someone an 'Average zoomer pajeet.'

articles.title: Pajeet Babus Ban Telegram to Prevent Cheating
articles.body:
Anon criticised the Pajeet Babus for banning Telegram to prevent cheating in exams, arguing that this approach does not address the root problem. Instead, they suggested that the authorities should focus on fixing the actual issue.

Anon further claimed that the government's actions are ineffective in preventing exam leaks, describing it as a form of 'water testing with censorship.' Another Anon described the government's actions as 'authoritarian' and alleged that they are acting on the orders of a 'Jewish master.' The ban on Telegram was questioned by Mallu Mogger, who pointed out that there are 'millions of other platforms to leak that shit.' This was in response to a claim that the paper had already leaked on Telegram.

A separate Anon expressed a wish for WhatsApp to be banned instead.

articles.title: Anon questions BJP links to sex work
articles.body:
Anon opened a thread asking how sex workers allegedly join the BJP and portray themselves as traditional girls to fool 'normies.' The poster claimed this erases their past and wondered why all of India's sex workers are from the BJP.

One respondent called the BJP a 'Gateway of whores for rehab.' Another suggested people change over time, asking if the original poster was the same person they were five years ago.

A third anon made a personal remark, saying the original poster's wife had slept with 100 men before marrying him.

The conversation included a remark about the director of 'The Kashmir Files' and a complaint about 'cuckolded' BJP supporters.

articles.title: Anon Worries About Indian Govt's Move
articles.body: Anon asked how the Indian government will cope with a certain development. Another anon replied that this is a move towards authoritarianism, citing the UK's recent law requiring government ID to use social media. A respondent asked what the problem is with showing government ID for using apps, suggesting there's nothing to hide. Anon also joked about using Telegram with a VPN anyway.
